completion_summary: Implemented dependency-free formal PPO/SAC action-policy contracts, exact task/checkpoint/split/hash verification, an executable formal adapter registry, deterministic masked-only cell binding, and explicit diagnostic-vs-formal resource scopes so training resources remain checkpoint-level. Validation: 27 focused tests, ruff, and mypy passed in the lock-bound SB3 2.9.0/Torch 2.13.0 environment; 38/38 RL regressions and 121/121 merged runner/preflight/matrix/resource/classic-RL regressions passed. Reference evidence: no reference_repos were used or modified; lockfile-bound SB3/Torch interfaces only, with licenses and applicability recorded. Limitations: no four-task training, Dev selection, or formal checkpoint is claimed; 40 preregistered training runs and 8 frozen checkpoints remain for the next slice, and the legacy flow-only gate correctly fails closed after backend-report drift. Final integrated implementation commit: fad60c42679f5fbb354f22507aeec57332c73ed6.
notes: Slice 1 of benchmark-v05-rl-adapters. This claim freezes the formal contract and adapter boundary only; it must not mark the parent todo complete or use Bench/reference feedback. Stable-Baselines3 2.9.0 and Torch 2.13.0 are lockfile-bound runtime dependencies; reference_repos remain read-only and unused.
scope: Establish the formal PPO/SAC method, checkpoint, allocation, replay, and split resource-accounting contract before the multi-task training slice, without claiming formal performance evidence.
